approach: Scrum with 2-week sprints, 6 sprints over 12 weeks. Decision gates between each major phase. Architectural decision points mapped to sprint boundaries.

phases.name: Discovery and Architecture Sign-Off
objectives: Validate architecture with payer technical team
Confirm Autonomize AI integration approach and API contracts
Establish development environment and CI/CD pipeline
Finalize LOB selection for Phase 1 (recommend: Commercial)
deliverables: Signed Architecture Decision Record (ADR)
Autonomize AI integration contract and API specifications
AWS infrastructure base (VPC, subnets, IAM roles, KMS keys)
CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions → ECR → ECS)
Development and staging environments provisioned
success_criteria: Architecture approved by CIO and CMO
Autonomize AI API access confirmed and tested
Infrastructure-as-code deploying cleanly to dev environment
Team onboarded and sprint 1 capacity confirmed
risks: Autonomize AI API contract negotiation delay

phases.name: Integration Build — Core Pipeline
objectives: Build ingestion pipeline (fax OCR, portal API, X12 278 parser)
Build FHIR R4 Facade with legacy data adapters
Build eligibility service (Facets integration)
Deploy Kafka event bus with PA request topics
Implement audit service with immutable DynamoDB trail
deliverables: Ingestion service processing all 3 channels (fax, portal, EDI)
FHIR R4 Facade with at least 2 EMR integrations
Eligibility service with Facets API integration
Kafka event bus with pa-requests, pa-determinations, audit-events topics
Audit service with complete decision logging
Data normalizer producing unified PA Request objects
success_criteria: End-to-end data flow: portal submission → Kafka → FHIR query → eligibility check
Fax OCR processing test documents with >95% accuracy on printed text
X12 278 transactions parsing correctly against ASC X12 test files
Audit trail capturing all events with correct hash chain
FHIR R4 client successfully querying test FHIR endpoint
dependencies: Sample fax documents for OCR testing

phases.name: AI Integration and Autonomize AI Platform
objectives: Integrate Autonomize AI PA Copilot with Kafka event pipeline
Configure multi-agent pipeline (Document → NLP → Eligibility → Guidelines → Decision)
Build guidelines matching service (InterQual/MCG integration)
Implement human review workflow (Step Functions)
Configure LOB rules engine for Commercial LOB
deliverables: PA Copilot processing PA requests from Kafka and producing determinations
Guidelines matching service with InterQual API integration
Human review workflow routing low-confidence cases to reviewer queue
LOB configuration engine with Commercial LOB rules loaded
Response router delivering determinations to portal and EDI
AI guardrails: prompt injection prevention, output validation, confidence thresholds
success_criteria: End-to-end PA determination: submission → AI review → determination → response
Confidence scoring working with 0.85 threshold routing to human review
Guidelines matching returning correct InterQual citations for test cases
Human reviewer receiving pre-assembled evidence package
Response router delivering determination in correct format per originating channel
dependencies: P-002 Kafka and FHIR Facade operational

phases.name: QA, UAT, and Security Hardening
objectives: Integration testing across all components
Performance testing at production scale (2.5M/month throughput)
Security hardening and HIPAA compliance verification
UAT with clinical SMEs using real-world PA scenarios
Penetration testing for cross-tenant and prompt injection
HIPAA breach tabletop exercise
deliverables: Integration test suite covering all 10 data flows
Performance test results demonstrating P95 < 30s at target load
Security assessment report from pen test vendor
UAT sign-off from clinical SMEs
HIPAA compliance checklist (all controls verified)
Breach response tabletop exercise report
Operational runbooks for incident response
success_criteria: All integration tests passing
P95 latency < 30s at 2x peak load (stress test)
No critical or high findings from pen test (or mitigated before go-live)
Clinical accuracy >= 95% concordance on UAT test cases
Breach tabletop exercise completed with < 1-hour notification workflow verified
dependencies: UAT test cases from clinical SMEs
risks: Pen test findings requiring significant remediation

phases.name: Go-Live and Hypercare
objectives: Production deployment (blue/green) for Commercial LOB
MLOps monitoring activation (drift detection, alerting)
Operational dashboards live
Hypercare support with rapid response to production issues
Go-live communication to provider network
deliverables: Production deployment completed and verified
MLOps pipeline active (SageMaker Model Monitor running)
QuickSight dashboards live for operations, clinical, and executive views
Hypercare support roster and escalation procedures
Go-live communication to Commercial LOB provider network
Phase 2 planning document (next LOB onboarding)
success_criteria: Production system processing live PA requests for Commercial LOB
Auto-determination rate tracking toward 60% target
No critical production incidents in first 48 hours
Monitoring alerting correctly on test scenarios
Dashboards showing real-time PA metrics

decision_gates.name: Architecture Sign-Off
criteria: Architecture reviewed and approved by CIO (Priya Patel)
Security architecture approved by Compliance (James Morrison)

options.conditional_go: Proceed with identified risks documented and mitigation plan
options.no_go: Revisit architecture. Re-engage Autonomize AI on contract terms.

decision_gates.name: Integration Checkpoint
criteria: End-to-end data flow operational in staging
All 3 ingestion channels processing test data
FHIR Facade connecting to at least 2 clinical data sources
Budget and timeline on track (< 15% variance)

options.conditional_go: Proceed with reduced scope (e.g., portal-only ingestion for go-live, fax in Phase 2)
options.no_go: Pause AI integration. Resolve integration blockers before proceeding.

decision_gates.name: Pre-Production Readiness
criteria: Pen test complete with no unresolved critical/high findings
HIPAA BAA with Autonomize AI executed
decision_gates.decider: Priya Patel (CIO) + Dr. Sarah Chen (CMO) + James Morrison (Compliance)

options.conditional_go: Deploy with limited auto-determination (higher confidence threshold) and increased monitoring
options.no_go: Delay go-live. Address critical findings. Re-test.

sprints.name: Foundation Sprint
sprints.goal: Infrastructure provisioned, CI/CD operational, architecture signed off
key_work_items: AWS CDK infrastructure deployment (VPC, subnets, IAM, KMS)
CI/CD pipeline: GitHub Actions → ECR → ECS blue/green
Kafka cluster (MSK) provisioned with PA topics
Autonomize AI API integration test (connectivity verified)
Architecture review meeting with payer technical team

sprints.name: Ingestion Sprint
sprints.goal: All 3 ingestion channels processing test data into Kafka
key_work_items: Document Processing Service: Textract OCR + Comprehend Medical NLP
X12 278 parser with ASC X12 grammar validation
Portal API with schema validation and OAuth 2.0
Data Normalizer: unified PA Request schema
Fax → S3 → Textract → Comprehend → Normalizer → Kafka pipeline

sprints.name: Clinical Data Sprint
sprints.goal: FHIR Facade operational, eligibility service live, Autonomize AI integration started
key_work_items: FHIR R4 Facade with SMART on FHIR auth
Legacy Data Adapter (first 2 legacy DB connectors)
Clinical Data Aggregator producing FHIR Bundles
Eligibility Service with Facets API integration
Autonomize AI PA Copilot Kafka consumer integration (start)

sprints.name: AI Decision Sprint
sprints.goal: End-to-end PA determination working in staging
key_work_items: PA Copilot multi-agent pipeline configuration
Guidelines Matching Service (InterQual API integration)
Decision Agent confidence scoring and threshold routing
Human Review Workflow (Step Functions)
Response Router (portal notification, X12 278 response)
Audit Service with immutable DynamoDB trail

sprints.name: QA and Security Sprint
sprints.goal: All integration tests passing, pen test complete, UAT started
key_work_items: Integration test suite (all 10 data flows)
Performance testing at 2x peak load
Security hardening: WAF rules, PHI log redaction verification
Pen test coordination and remediation
UAT clinical scenarios with SMEs
HIPAA compliance checklist verification

sprints.name: Go-Live Sprint
sprints.goal: Production deployment, monitoring active, Commercial LOB live
key_work_items: UAT sign-off and remediation
Breach tabletop exercise
MLOps pipeline activation (SageMaker Model Monitor)
QuickSight dashboards deployment
Blue/green production deployment
Hypercare monitoring and rapid response
Phase 2 planning: next LOB selection

critical_path: FHIR R4 Facade with clinical data access (P-002)

risk_register.risk: Autonomize AI API integration more complex than anticipated
risk_register.mitigation: Start integration in Week 3 (Sprint 2). Dedicate 2 AI/ML engineers full-time. Autonomize AI provides integration support engineer.
risk_register.trigger: API contract not finalized by end of Week 2

risk_register.risk: Legacy clinical DB connectors require custom schema mapping per EMR vendor
risk_register.mitigation: Scope Phase 1 to 2 EMR integrations. Build adapter pattern for easy addition. Remaining EMRs in Phase 2.
risk_register.trigger: More than 3 unique legacy schemas discovered during Sprint 3

risk_register.risk: Fax OCR accuracy below 95% target on handwritten clinical notes
risk_register.mitigation: Use Textract for printed text (>95% accuracy). Route handwritten documents to human review. Measure accuracy early in Sprint 2.
risk_register.trigger: OCR accuracy < 90% on test document set

risk_register.risk: Clinical SMEs unavailable at critical points (UAT, guidelines validation)
risk_register.mitigation: Schedule SME availability in advance. Identify backup SMEs. Pre-build UAT test case library.

risk_register.risk: Pen test findings require significant remediation delaying go-live
risk_register.mitigation: Engage pen test vendor by Week 7 (not Week 9). Run preliminary security scan in Sprint 4. Address findings in Sprint 5.
risk_register.trigger: More than 2 high-severity findings from pen test

risk_register.risk: Clinical accuracy below 95% concordance target at UAT
risk_register.mitigation: Start accuracy testing in Sprint 4 with sample cases. Iteratively tune guidelines matching. Increase confidence threshold if accuracy lags. Conditional go-live with higher threshold.

risk_register.risk: Autonomize AI platform outage during integration testing
risk_register.mitigation: Build human-fallback workflow early (Sprint 4). Design integration to gracefully degrade. Include SLA requirements in contract.

external.dependency: Autonomize AI API contract and access credentials
Facets API documentation and test environment access
FHIR R4 test endpoint access (at least 2 EMR systems)
InterQual API access and license confirmation
Sample fax documents for OCR testing (50+ documents)

communication_plan.stakeholder_updates: Weekly status report (Mondays) to CIO, CMO, VP Ops, and Compliance Director. Includes: sprint progress, risks, decisions needed, budget tracking.
demo_schedule: Week 2: Architecture walkthrough and infrastructure demo → CIO, CMO
Week 4: Ingestion pipeline demo (fax OCR, portal, EDI processing) → VP Ops, CIO
Week 6: Integration checkpoint — end-to-end data flow demo → All stakeholders
Week 8: AI determination demo — live PA processing in staging → CMO, VP Ops, Clinical SMEs
Week 10: UAT results and security assessment briefing → CIO, CMO, Compliance
Week 12: Go-live demo and production metrics → All stakeholders + executive leadership
